feature: Inventory and dependency graph: log source enrollment / cost
user_story: As an operator, I can understand and act on log source enrollment cost posture without leaving Mayyam.
ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for log source enrollment: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover log source enrollment, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the cost pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for log source enrollment.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for log source enrollment.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Log Management and Analytics log source enrollment inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: log-source-enrollment_cost_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for log source enrollment cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for log source enrollment; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for log source enrollment.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for log source enrollment, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for log source enrollment.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for log source enrollment.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing log source enrollment data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling log source enrollment inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for log source enrollment.
acceptance_criteria: Given log source enrollment data exists, when the cost inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: log source enrollment connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

implementation_detail: Create repeatable runbooks and scheduled checks for live tail security posture.
backend_scope: Add runbook schema, scheduler hooks, history, and notification events for live tail.
frontend_scope: Add runbook editor, schedule controls, history table, and owner assignment for live tail.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Log Management and Analytics live tail automation with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: live-tail_security_automation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for live tail security that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for live tail; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for live tail.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for live tail recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for live tail.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for live tail.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing live tail data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling live tail automation broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for live tail.
acceptance_criteria: Given live tail data exists, when the security automation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: live tail connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for log search: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Let an agent investigate log search operational-excellence issues with bounded tool use, read-only diagnostics first, explicit uncertainty, and approval-gated mutations.
backend_scope: Add tool registry, planner, execution trace, read-only default policy, token/time budgets, rollback plan schema, and stop conditions for log search.
frontend_scope: Add investigation timeline, tool-call evidence, confidence labels, approval prompts, replay, and promote-to-runbook actions for log search.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Log Management and Analytics log search agentic-investigation with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: log-search_operational-excellence_agentic-investigation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for log search operational-excellence that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for log search; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for log search.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for log search recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for log search.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for log search.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing log search data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling log search agentic-investigation broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for log search.
acceptance_criteria: Given log search data exists, when the operational-excellence agentic-investigation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: log search connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
